channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: India's richest person wants to build the world's largest data center, five times the capacity of Microsoft's biggest site
item.description: Mukesh Ambani wants to build the world's largest data center, five times the capacity of Microsoft's mega site.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Only two weeks in and AI phenomenon DeepSeek is officially growing faster than ChatGPT
item.description: Exclusive Similarweb data reveals ChatGPT growth might have peaked, and, though it’s early days, a new competitor could be in line for its throne.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried’s parents appealing to Trump to pardon their beloved son: report
item.description: The mother of Sam Bankman-Fried has apparently had a major change of heart about Trump after once leading a top Silicon Valley fundraising network for anti-Trump Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: A new Chrome browser highjacking attack could affect billions of users - here's how to fight it
item.description: There's a new highjacking attack that could take over your Chrome browser and steal all your data - here's how to keep your computer safe.
item.dc:creator: allisa.james@futurenet.com (Allisa James), TechRadar

item.title: I've been hunting for a wet-floor cleaner that can actually clean to the edges of rooms, and Dreame might have just come up with a solution
item.description: The new Dreame H15 Pro is impressively maneuverable for a wet-dry cleaner, and packs some innovative smart features.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google offers ‘voluntary exit’ for any US employees working on Pixel, Android
item.description: Google is asking its Platforms & Devices workers, including Android and Pixel, to consider resigning.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hoping for new desktop CPUs from Intel this year? I hate to break it to you, but it still very much sounds like they won’t arrive until 2026 with Nova Lake
item.description: One of Intel’s interim co-CEOs has dropped some clues about what kind of CPUs we’re getting in the near future.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I can't wait to watch all the lies unravel in the scandalous Netflix series Apple Cider Vinegar after seeing a new clip
item.description: Apple Cider Vinegar's latest clip is a reenactment of a real-life TV interview that started to unravel Belle Gibson's scam.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: YouTube Premium subscribers just got 5 handy new tricks – but I'm still waiting for this one feature
item.description: Premium users can now ramp up YouTube speeds to 4x and get higher quality audio, but there's still room for improvement.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cómo DeepSeek cambia la ecuación de la IA generativa para los CIO
item.description: La empresa emergente china de inteligencia artificial (IA) DeepSeek causó sensación la semana pasada cuando dio a conocer una versión de código abierto de su modelo de razonamiento, DeepSeek-R1, que afirma tener un rendimiento superior al modelo generativo preentrenado (GPT) o1 de OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: HP redefines PC refresh approach using performance data
item.description: Traditionally, a PC refresh plan is scheduled based on the age of the devices, but as technology advances, so do opportunities. PCs today often continue to perform as needed for much longer than established in a lifecycle policy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bringing zero downtime to anywhere work
item.description: In a work-from-anywhere world, how can a team of so few be expected to keep doing so much for so many? The pace is unsustainable. It’s time for IT to enlist some support of its own and delegate—handing off time-consuming tasks to people and to intelligent, automated tools.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: After a week of DeepSeek freakout, doubts and mysteries remain
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: RFK Jr. kept asking to see the science that vaccines were safe. After he saw it, he dismissed it
item.description: The man who hopes to be President Donald Trump’s health secretary says he needs to see data showing vaccines are safe, but he dismissed the evidence shown by an influential Republican senator.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft posts 10% growth for Q4 as it plans to spend $80 billion on AI
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla’s Q4 results fell short of Wall Street’s forecasts. Here’s why
item.description: Tesla’s fourth-quarter adjusted profits rose slightly amid a big push to sell its electric vehicles with offers of zero financing and other incentives, but the results still fell short of Wall Street forecasts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: John Mulaney dunked on Meghan Markle and Prince Harry at star-studded Netflix event
item.description: The comedian poked fun at Meghan and Harry's Netflix track record, shortly after the duchess announced she was delaying the release of her lifestyle show in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Big Tech brushes off DeepSeek threat
item.description: Leaders of major U.S. tech companies at the forefront of artificial intelligence (AI) are brushing off a popular new intelligence model from Chinese startup DeepSeek, despite fears it could upend the industry.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Timeline of HPE’s $14 billion bid for Juniper
item.description: AI is a key factor in one of the tech industry’s most significant M&A developments: HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. The $14 billion deal would shake up the industry, and regulatory watchdogs in the U.S. and worldwide have raised antitrust concerns.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AI investments face integration, compliance, and skills challenges: survey
item.description: Network engineers report that they see the current and future value in using artificial intelligence for network management and cybersecurity purposes, according to new survey results, but they also anticipate more barriers to successful adoption than their C-suite counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CompTIA unveils AI Essentials training resource
item.description: Artificial intelligence is changing how work gets done, creating new technology roles, and transforming the entire employment landscape.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft’s new DocumentDB rethinks NoSQL on PostgreSQL
item.description: Microsoft’s recent launch of a standalone version of the MongoDB compatibility layer for its global-scale Azure Cosmos DB brought back an old name. Back in 2018, when the company unveiled a public version of the Project Florence database engine that powers much of Azure, they called it DocumentDB.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Verizon brings AI suite to enterprise infrastructure customers
item.description: Verizon Business has launched AI Connect, an integrated suite of products designed to let businesses deploy generative artificial intelligence (AI) workloads at scale.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need
item.description: The government's personnel shop has devised a five-part strategy for planning, hiring and retaining cybersecurity people. But not everyone follows the strategy. The post How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025
item.description: Last year was an eventful one on the cybersecurity front. But big cyber developments in the federal scape are expected in 2025. The post Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US Government sued after mass emails to federal workforce allegedly sent from insecure server
item.description: When officials working for the incoming Trump administration decided they wanted to email the entire federal workforce last week, they didn’t hang about.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US officials probe China’s DeepSeek AI amid security and privacy scrutiny
item.description: The Chinese AI app DeepSeek has come under intense scrutiny from both the US and European regulators, raising alarms over national security risks, data privacy concerns, and potential intellectual property theft.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fake toll road texts sweep America as Chinese scammers target US drivers
item.description: Toll road text scam: Fake messages claim unpaid fees, seek payment via fraudulent links. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this scam is becoming increasingly sophisticated and widespread.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A distributed state of mind: Event-driven multi-agent systems
item.description: While large language models are useful for chatbots, Q&A systems, translation, and other language tasks, their real power emerges when they can act on insights, automating a broader range of problems. In other words, we unlock their greatest potential when we tap their reasoning capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
